results.title: PhD Internship – AI Processor Architecture for Edge Applications – Germany – NXP Semiconductors Germany GmbH Zentrale – Michael Abbas Blog
highlights:
PhD Internship – AI Processor Architecture for Edge Applications – Germany – NXP Semiconductors Germany GmbH Zentrale – Michael Abbas Blog
...
Join a vibrant, international R&D team at NXP Semiconductors, where academic excellence meets real-world impact. Our team includes multiple PhD-level engineers and a university lecturer, all working at the forefront of AI processor architecture for edge applications. We're shaping the future of embedded intelligence in domains like robotics, automotive (ADAS), and industrial automation.
...
In this role, your research will contribute to both academic advancement and the development of technologies that power billions of AI-enabled edge devices. You'll collaborate with fellow PhD students, Technical Directors, and NXP Fellows—our technological thought leaders. You'll also gain hands-on experience with real silicon and state-of-the-art industrial software stacks for machine learning.
...
### Research Focus (flexible within scope):
...
- Heterogeneous accelerator architectures for robotics, ADAS (e.g., radar, lidar), and industrial AI
- Co-design of hardware, software, and tools for real-time, safety-critical systems
- Domain-specific compiler and runtime support for physical AI workloads
- Integration of sensor fusion and perception algorithms into custom accelerators

- PhD student in Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field
- Enrolled at a university that supports internships during the PhD
- Strong interest in real-time systems, embedded AI, and heterogeneous computing
- Experience with hardware design (e.g., RTL, HLS, FPGA, ASIC)
- Familiarity with AI workloads in physical or embedded systems is a plus
...
If you're passionate about AI hardware and want your research to reach millions of users through real products, this is your chance to make a tangible impact.

results.title: Alisa Morozova
highlights:
AI/ML Engineer @ NXP Semiconductors | Electrical and Electronics Engineering
...
AI ML engineer at [NXP Semiconductors](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nxp-semiconductors)
...
As an AI/ML Engineer at NXP Semiconductors, I work on cutting-edge projects leveraging my expertise in Python programming and energy systems. My academic journey includes a Master's degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering at Delft University of Technology and a Bachelor's degree from Eindhoven University of Technology, emphasizing my dedication to advancing sustainable energy solutions. During my recent tenure at TNO, I contributed to developing a co-simulation framework for managing electric vehicle charging integrated with renewable energy sources. My role involved creating Python-based control logic, simulating dynamic energy flows, and visualizing energy metrics to support real-time optimization. I am motivated by innovation and teamwork in optimizing energy systems for a sustainable future.
...
### AI ML engineer at [NXP Semiconductors](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nxp-semiconductors) (Current)
...
, operates in 6

...
Currently working on a co-simulation framework for managing electric vehicle charging in combination with solar and wind energy production, battery storage, and grid constraints. The project focuses on real-time control algorithms that optimize energy use while respecting voltage limits, market prices, and EV user behavior. My responsibilities include: Developing and integrating Python-based control logic for smart EV charging across multiple vehicles. Simulating dynamic energy flows using data from solar panels, wind turbines, and household consumption. Modeling and managing energy storage systems with price-based decision-making. Creating automated plots and visualizations for battery levels, voltage profiles, and financial outcomes. Working with modular, scalable architecture for coupling components like heat pumps, grid behavior, and weather input. This project combines machine learning readiness, energy systems modeling, and optimization, laying the foundation for smarter and more sustainable charging infrastructures. Show less

In a few years, training an AI model will be as common for companies as writing lines of code. AI is software 2.0! Today marks an incredible breakthrough that we are thrilled to share with you all! 🎉 

Just 4 months ago, pre-training Large Language Models (LLMs) seemed like a mountainous task reserved only for large corporations or well-endowed university programs. But not anymore!
...
We pre-trained a 22B LLM, and it's now LIVE on HuggingFace! 🎊
...
Achieving this feat was not a walk in the park, but a testament to the phenomenal strides the #OpenSource community has taken recently. We are immensely grateful to be part of such an innovative and collaborative community that constantly pushes boundaries. #NucleusAI #HuggingFace

Activity: Gilles Meyhi liked this
...
Exciting news! We released XTTS open-sourced. It is the best open-source  model we've released so far.
...
XTTS uses the latest generative AI techniques to deliver faster, higher-quality speech in 13 different languages. It is now accessible with 🐸TTS.
...
XTTS offers…
...
- Voice cloning with just a 3-second audioclip.
- Emotion and style transfer by cloning.
- Cross-language voice cloning.
- Multi-lingual speech generation.
- 24khz sampling rate.
...
As of now, XTTS-v1 supports the following languages: 

English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Polish, Turkish, Russian, Dutch, Czech, Arabic, and Chinese
...
To start using XTTS, all you need to do is ** pip install TTS.**

As one of the CDO MT members I have the responsiblity to as a Lead Data Architect to prepare and support all data related initiatives for NXP in becoming a more data insights driven organization from a data architectural perspective. This is done with the support of all (Enterprise) Architects within the company and in close collaboration with all other CDO lead roles, Data Governance, Data Portfolio, Data Platform, Data Science. At NXP, I work on building and scaling data and analytics systems that support high performance computing workloads across global engineering teams. I design and operate data pipelines and analytics platforms on AWS to process large scale HPC and telemetry data, enabling reliable and fast insights for over 2,000 users across 15+ clusters. My work focuses on improving performance, reliability, and usability of data products. I have reduced dashboard latency significantly through query optimization and data model improvements, and improved pipeline reliability by resolving ingestion issues, implementing validation logic, and stabilizing production data sources. I have delivered and maintained multiple production dashboards using Tableau, Shiny, and Splunk, covering workload analytics, capacity planning, and system performance. These dashboards are used by engineering and operations teams to monitor system efficiency and make data driven decisions. Beyond analytics, I work on automation and platform improvements. This includes building monitoring and alerting workflows, automating operational processes, and improving CI pipelines to reduce manual effort and increase system stability. I am also involved in applied machine learning initiatives, including feature engineering and model experimentation for predicting project delays and improving system level predictions. This includes working with clustering techniques, vectorization methods, and model evaluation frameworks. My work sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and platform reliability, with a strong focus on building systems that are practical, scalable, and used in real production environments.

As high school graduation neared, I thought for sure I wanted to become a doctor. Feeling more drawn to engineering I ended up changing my mind and instead of becoming a doctor, I wanted to become the person who would be responsible for innovating tools to facilitate the job of a doctor. Hence, I started studying Biomedical Engineering. It was there I first came in contact with coding using Python. This was also an initial turning point in my studies where I discovered how much I enjoyed coding and solving problems with computer programs. From there on I continued developing my coding skills throughout different courses. I tried things from Android app development using Java, to finally writing a segmentation algorithm using neural networks for my final master thesis. What specifically sparked an interest in my studies was using machine learning to facilitate processes in healthcare. Be it through creating predictive models or writing automated segmentation algorithms. Machine learning is continuously changing at a rapid speed, which continuously pushes me to develop and improve on new skills. I have learned a lot during my studies, but still feel there’s always new things to learn about. This continuous eagerness originates from the years I have been leisurely playing piano. results.title: NXP’s Neural Axis Architecture: A Blueprint to Own the Robotic Nervous System - Futurum
highlights:
At COMPUTEX 2026,
...
XP CEO Rafael
...
otomayor unveiled the N
...
reasoning, coordination, and reflexive intelligence. The strategy can position
...
XP as the NVIDIA of the extreme edge, owning the full stack of silicon, deterministic networking, and software that improve real-world robots not just at reasoning but reacting. The question is whether
...
can build a
...
-class moat at the
...
through reinforcement learning, as NVIDIA has done
...
The News: At COMPUTEX 2026, NXP Semiconductors President and CEO Rafael Sotomayor delivered a physical AI keynote introducing the company’s Neural Axis architecture, a framework that distributes intelligence across three interconnected layers — reasoning, coordination, and reflexive intelligence — so autonomous machines can operate safely in dynamic environments. Sotomayor argued that the defining challenge of physical AI is not raw intelligence but the ability to react in milliseconds without round-tripping to the cloud. NXP demonstrated the architecture across drones, software-defined vehicles, and humanoid robots, paired it with a trust framework spanning containment, protection, verification, and adaptation, and tied it to its eIQ toolkit, its $307M acquisition of edge AI NPU maker Kinara, and a foundational partnership with NVIDIA.
...
reinforcing.
...
that seed the
...
The deepest part of NXP’s pitch is the claim that synthetic data alone does not work for physical AI, much resembling the shift to RL for LLM post-training. When robots encounter genuinely novel situations, a VLA model applies an action, may get it wrong at first, and can learn in context. The refinement that turns a clumsy demo into a deployable product comes from real reinforcement learning across deployed fleets, effectively crowdsourcing experience from the physical edge. In that loop, the deployed device becomes the data-generating engine, and the resulting models become the key IP. This is the edge analog of NVIDIA’s most durable advantage: not the chip, but the installed base and developer gravity that compound over time. If NXP’s silicon is what makes low-latency, trustworthy, always-on real-world learning possible, then the more robots ship on Neural Axis, the better the models get, and the more builders standardize on the platform. NXP’s embrace of frontier robotics research makes close collaboration and lock-in with the developer community possible.
...
The fulfillment of this vision will depend on the maturation of the software layer to make frontier intelligence compatible with edge processors. eIQ is promising but not irreplaceable, and the edge AI tooling world is fragmented across Qualcomm, NXP, STMicroelectronics, Renesas, Texas Instruments, and a long tail of open-source runtimes. NXP partnered with NVIDIA in integrating the Holoscan Sensor Bridge, inviting the question of whether NXP is building a rival platform or a complement that NVIDIA can absorb by pushing Jetson and Holoscan further down toward the reflex layer. The open-source momentum behind VLA and world models could also erode the proprietary value of RL fine-tuning if the best models become free. The most honest framing is that NXP has assembled the pieces of an NVIDIA-of-the-edge platform and articulated the strategy clearly — but the moat still has to be earned in design wins and developer adoption. With robotics searching for its ChatGPT moment, the window for definitive platforms has opened.
...
Brendan is Research Director, Semiconductors, Supply Chain, and Emerging Tech. He advises clients on strategic initiatives and leads the Futurum Semiconductors Practice. He is an experienced tech industry analyst who has guided tech leaders in identifying market opportunities spanning edge processors, generative AI applications, and hyperscale data centers.

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
Aug 2026
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- A unique position at the interface of an AI platform team and a world-class neuroscience research center, with exposure to both engineering and scientific challenges.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

Senior AI and LLM Engineer | Hirevector

# Senior AI and LLM Engineer
...
NVIDIA’s Silicon Co-Design Group – Efficiency (SCGE) team is seeking a Senior AI/LLM Engineer to innovate, develop, and integrate advanced AI technologies into our design and automation infrastructure. In this role, you will architect and implement solutions that improve the efficiency, scalability, and intelligence of our workflows. The ideal candidate blends deep technical expertise with practical, hands-on experience, driving every stage of AI solution planning, design, and implementation. At NVIDIA, we strive for perfection, encourage innovation, and provide opportunities to explore new ways to succeed! What you'll be doing: Designing and implementing AI/LLM-powered systems to improve automation, design verification, and workflow efficiency. Collaborating with multi-functional engineering teams to find opportunities for AI integration and performance optimization. Developing and fine-tuning large language models tailored to semiconductor design and productivity applications. Evaluating new frameworks, architectures, and tools to improve efficiencies powered by artificial intelligence across the organization. Tracking and measuring AI impact using data-driven metrics to continuously refine solutions. What we need to see: MS in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience) and 8+ years of development experience, with at least 4 years focused on AI/ML, LLMs, or generative AI. Strong proficiency in Python and deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow. Proven track record of deploying scalable AI/ML models in production. Ability to balance multiple simultaneous projects Excellent problem-solving, communication, and collaboration skills. Ways to Standout from the crowd: Experience with EDA, semiconductor workflows, or design automation systems. Publications or open-source contributions demonstrating deep expertise in LLMs or AI infrastructure. Familiarity with multimodal or reinforcement learning applications. Ability to translate innovative AI research into practical, high-impact production tools. NVIDIA is widely considered to be one of the world's most desirable employers in the technology field. We have some of the most forward-thinking and hardworking people in the world working for us. If you're creative and autonomous, we want to hear from you! #LI-Hybrid Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 184,000 USD - 287,500 USD for Level 4, and 224,000 USD - 356,500 USD for Level 5. You will also be eligible for equity and benefits . Applications for this job will be accepted at least until April 14, 2026. This posting is for an existing vacancy. NVIDIA uses AI tools in its recruiting processes. NVIDIA is committed to fostering a diverse work environment and proud to be an equal opportunity employer. As we highly value diversity in our current and future employees, we do not discriminate (including in our hiring and promotion practices) on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any other characteristic protected by law.
